1→use std::str::FromStr;
     2→
     3→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     4→
     5→/// A project registered in the Factory, representing a local codebase that can
     6→/// have issues managed on the Kanban board and executed by the agent pipeline.
     7→///
     8→/// Projects are created via the Factory API and stored in SQLite. Each project
     9→/// has an associated filesystem path that the pipeline uses when checking out
    10→/// worktrees and running `forge` commands.
    11→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
    12→pub struct Project {
    13→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
    14→    pub id: i64,
    15→    /// Human-readable name shown in the Factory UI.
    16→    pub name: String,
    17→    /// Absolute filesystem path to the project root on the host machine.
    18→    pub path: String,
    19→    /// Optional GitHub repository slug (`owner/repo`) used for auto-PR creation.
    20→    pub github_repo: Option<String>,
    21→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when the project was registered.
    22→    pub created_at: String,
    23→}
    24→
    25→/// The column an issue currently occupies in the Kanban board.
    26→///
    27→/// Columns define the workflow lifecycle of an issue. Moving an issue to
    28→/// `InProgress` triggers automatic pipeline execution via [`crate::factory::pipeline::PipelineRunner`].
    29→/// The typical progression is:
    30→///
    31→/// `Backlog` → `Ready` → `InProgress` → `InReview` → `Done`
    32→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
    33→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    34→pub enum IssueColumn {
    35→    /// Work that has been captured but is not yet scheduled for development.
    36→    Backlog,
    37→    /// Scheduled and groomed work that is ready to be picked up by the pipeline.
    38→    Ready,
    39→    /// Issue is currently being implemented by the agent pipeline.
    40→    ///
    41→    /// Moving into this column triggers a [`PipelineRun`] to be created and
    42→    /// execution to begin via [`crate::factory::pipeline::PipelineRunner`].
    43→    InProgress,
    44→    /// Implementation is complete and awaiting human or automated review.
    45→    InReview,
    46→    /// Work is accepted and complete. Terminal state.
    47→    Done,
    48→}
    49→
    50→impl IssueColumn {
    51→    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
    52→        match self {
    53→            Self::Backlog => "backlog",
    54→            Self::Ready => "ready",
    55→            Self::InProgress => "in_progress",
    56→            Self::InReview => "in_review",
    57→            Self::Done => "done",
    58→        }
    59→    }
    60→}
    61→
    62→impl FromStr for IssueColumn {
    63→    type Err = String;
    64→
    65→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
    66→        match s {
    67→            "backlog" => Ok(Self::Backlog),
    68→            "ready" => Ok(Self::Ready),
    69→            "in_progress" => Ok(Self::InProgress),
    70→            "in_review" => Ok(Self::InReview),
    71→            "done" => Ok(Self::Done),
    72→            _ => Err(format!("Invalid column: {}", s)),
    73→        }
    74→    }
    75→}
    76→
    77→/// Priority level of a Kanban issue.
    78→///
    79→/// Used to communicate urgency to the agent pipeline and to sort issues within
    80→/// a column. The pipeline does not alter its behavior based on priority today,
    81→/// but the value is surfaced in the UI and stored for future scheduling heuristics.
    82→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
    83→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    84→pub enum Priority {
    85→    /// Cosmetic or nice-to-have work with no time pressure.
    86→    Low,
    87→    /// Default priority for standard feature work.
    88→    Medium,
    89→    /// Important work that should be addressed soon.
    90→    High,
    91→    /// Urgent issue requiring immediate attention, such as a production incident.
    92→    Critical,
    93→}
    94→
    95→impl Priority {
    96→    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
    97→        match self {
    98→            Self::Low => "low",
    99→            Self::Medium => "medium",
   100→            Self::High => "high",
   101→            Self::Critical => "critical",
   102→        }
   103→    }
   104→}
   105→
   106→impl FromStr for Priority {
   107→    type Err = String;
   108→
   109→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   110→        match s {
   111→            "low" => Ok(Self::Low),
   112→            "medium" => Ok(Self::Medium),
   113→            "high" => Ok(Self::High),
   114→            "critical" => Ok(Self::Critical),
   115→            _ => Err(format!("Invalid priority: {}", s)),
   116→        }
   117→    }
   118→}
   119→
   120→/// A Kanban issue representing a unit of work to be implemented by the agent pipeline.
   121→///
   122→/// Issues are the primary work items in the Factory. They are displayed on the
   123→/// Kanban board and move through columns as they progress. When an issue is moved
   124→/// to [`IssueColumn::InProgress`], the Factory API triggers a [`PipelineRun`] that
   125→/// invokes `forge` to implement the issue autonomously.
   126→///
   127→/// Issues can optionally be linked to a GitHub issue via `github_issue_number`.
   128→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   129→pub struct Issue {
   130→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   131→    pub id: i64,
   132→    /// Foreign key to the [`Project`] this issue belongs to.
   133→    pub project_id: i64,
   134→    /// Short summary of the work, shown as the card title on the board.
   135→    pub title: String,
   136→    /// Full specification of the work, passed as the prompt to the pipeline.
   137→    pub description: String,
   138→    /// Current Kanban column the issue occupies.
   139→    pub column: IssueColumn,
   140→    /// Sort order within the column. Lower values appear higher on the board.
   141→    pub position: i32,
   142→    /// Urgency level used for triage and display ordering.
   143→    pub priority: Priority,
   144→    /// Arbitrary string tags for filtering and categorisation (e.g. `["bug", "ui"]`).
   145→    pub labels: Vec<String>,
   146→    /// GitHub issue number if this card was synced from or linked to a GitHub issue.
   147→    pub github_issue_number: Option<i64>,
   148→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when the issue was created.
   149→    pub created_at: String,
   150→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent update to this issue.
   151→    pub updated_at: String,
   152→}
   153→
   154→/// State machine for a [`PipelineRun`].
   155→///
   156→/// Transitions follow the path: `Queued` → `Running` → `Completed` | `Failed` | `Cancelled`.
   157→/// Terminal states are identified by [`PipelineStatus::is_terminal`].
   158→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
   159→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   160→pub enum PipelineStatus {
   161→    /// The run has been created and is waiting for an executor slot.
   162→    Queued,
   163→    /// The pipeline is actively executing phases via `forge`.
   164→    Running,
   165→    /// All phases finished successfully. Terminal state.
   166→    Completed,
   167→    /// The pipeline encountered an unrecoverable error. Terminal state.
   168→    Failed,
   169→    /// The run was explicitly cancelled before completion. Terminal state.
   170→    Cancelled,
   171→}
   172→
   173→impl PipelineStatus {
   174→    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   175→        match self {
   176→            Self::Queued => "queued",
   177→            Self::Running => "running",
   178→            Self::Completed => "completed",
   179→            Self::Failed => "failed",
   180→            Self::Cancelled => "cancelled",
   181→        }
   182→    }
   183→}
   184→
   185→impl FromStr for PipelineStatus {
   186→    type Err = String;
   187→
   188→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   189→        match s {
   190→            "queued" => Ok(Self::Queued),
   191→            "running" => Ok(Self::Running),
   192→            "completed" => Ok(Self::Completed),
   193→            "failed" => Ok(Self::Failed),
   194→            "cancelled" => Ok(Self::Cancelled),
   195→            _ => Err(format!("Invalid pipeline status: {}", s)),
   196→        }
   197→    }
   198→}
   199→
   200→impl PipelineStatus {
   201→    /// Returns true for terminal states that indicate the pipeline is no longer running.
   202→    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {
   203→        matches!(self, Self::Completed | Self::Failed | Self::Cancelled)
   204→    }
   205→}

   207→/// A single execution of the Forge pipeline triggered by an issue moving to `InProgress`.
   208→///
   209→/// Each `PipelineRun` corresponds to one invocation of `forge` against a specific
   210→/// [`Issue`]. The run tracks progress through phases, the current iteration within
   211→/// a phase, and the outcome (summary, error, PR URL). When agent-team execution is
   212→/// enabled, the run is associated with an [`AgentTeam`] via `team_id`.
   213→///
   214→/// Runs are persisted in SQLite and their state is broadcast to connected WebSocket
   215→/// clients so the UI can display live progress.
   216→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   217→pub struct PipelineRun {
   218→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   219→    pub id: i64,
   220→    /// Foreign key to the [`Issue`] that triggered this run.
   221→    pub issue_id: i64,
   222→    /// Current lifecycle state of the run.
   223→    pub status: PipelineStatus,
   224→    /// Total number of phases in the spec being executed, once known.
   225→    pub phase_count: Option<i32>,
   226→    /// Index of the phase currently being executed (1-based).
   227→    pub current_phase: Option<i32>,
   228→    /// Current iteration number within the active phase.
   229→    pub iteration: Option<i32>,
   230→    /// Human-readable summary produced at the end of a successful run.
   231→    pub summary: Option<String>,
   232→    /// Error message captured when the run transitions to `Failed`.
   233→    pub error: Option<String>,
   234→    /// Git branch created for this run (e.g. `forge/issue-42`).
   235→    pub branch_name: Option<String>,
   236→    /// URL of the pull request opened after a successful run, if any.
   237→    pub pr_url: Option<String>,
   238→    /// Foreign key to the [`AgentTeam`] coordinating this run, if agent-team mode is active.
   239→    #[serde(default)]
   240→    pub team_id: Option<i64>,
   241→    /// `true` when this run is being executed by an agent team rather than a single pipeline.
   242→    #[serde(default)]
   243→    pub has_team: bool,
   244→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when execution began.
   245→    pub started_at: String,
   246→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when the run reached a terminal state, if applicable.
   247→    pub completed_at: Option<String>,
   248→}
   249→
   250→/// Status of a pipeline phase.
   251→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   252→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   253→pub enum PhaseStatus {
   254→    /// Phase has not started yet.
   255→    Pending,
   256→    /// Phase is currently executing iterations.
   257→    Running,
   258→    /// Phase finished successfully (agent emitted `<promise>DONE</promise>`).
   259→    Completed,
   260→    /// Phase exceeded its iteration budget or encountered an error.
   261→    Failed,
   262→}
   263→
   264→impl std::fmt::Display for PhaseStatus {
   265→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   266→        match self {
   267→            Self::Pending => write!(f, "pending"),
   268→            Self::Running => write!(f, "running"),
   269→            Self::Completed => write!(f, "completed"),
   270→            Self::Failed => write!(f, "failed"),
   271→        }
   272→    }
   273→}
   274→
   275→impl std::str::FromStr for PhaseStatus {
   276→    type Err = anyhow::Error;
   277→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   278→        match s {
   279→            "pending" => Ok(Self::Pending),
   280→            "running" => Ok(Self::Running),
   281→            "completed" => Ok(Self::Completed),
   282→            "failed" => Ok(Self::Failed),
   283→            other => anyhow::bail!("Invalid phase status: {}", other),
   284→        }
   285→    }
   286→}
   287→
   288→/// A single named phase within a [`PipelineRun`], tracking iteration progress.
   289→///
   290→/// Phases map directly to the phases defined in the Forge spec file (`.forge/spec.md`).
   291→/// Each phase has an iteration budget; the pipeline retries the phase until the agent
   292→/// emits `<promise>DONE</promise>` or the budget is exhausted.
   293→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   294→pub struct PipelinePhase {
   295→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   296→    pub id: i64,
   297→    /// Foreign key to the owning [`PipelineRun`].

   299→    /// Hierarchical phase identifier from the spec (e.g. `"1"`, `"2.1"`).
   300→    pub phase_number: String,
   301→    /// Human-readable name of the phase (e.g. `"Implement feature"`).
   302→    pub phase_name: String,
   303→    /// Current execution state of this phase.
   304→    pub status: PhaseStatus,
   305→    /// Current iteration count within this phase (incremented on each retry).
   306→    pub iteration: Option<i32>,
   307→    /// Maximum number of iterations allowed for this phase before it is marked failed.
   308→    pub budget: Option<i32>,
   309→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when this phase began executing.
   310→    pub started_at: Option<String>,
   311→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when this phase reached a terminal state.
   312→    pub completed_at: Option<String>,
   313→    /// Error message if the phase failed.
   314→    pub error: Option<String>,
   315→}

   379→
   380→/// Isolation strategy for agent task execution.
   381→///
   382→/// Controls how each [`AgentTask`] within a team is isolated from others to
   383→/// prevent file-system conflicts when multiple agents work in parallel.
   384→///
   385→/// Currently only `Worktree` and `Shared` are fully implemented.
   386→/// `Container` and `Hybrid` are reserved for future use.
   387→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   388→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   389→pub enum IsolationStrategy {
   390→    /// Each agent task operates in a dedicated `git worktree` branched from `main`.
   391→    /// Results are merged back after each wave completes.
   392→    Worktree,
   393→    /// Each agent task runs in an isolated Docker container. Reserved for future use.
   394→    Container,
   395→    /// Combines container and worktree isolation. Reserved for future use.
   396→    Hybrid,
   397→    /// All agent tasks share the same working directory. Suitable for sequential strategies
   398→    /// or when tasks are guaranteed to touch non-overlapping files.
   399→    Shared,
   400→}
   401→
   402→impl IsolationStrategy {
   403→    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   404→        match self {
   405→            Self::Worktree => "worktree",
   406→            Self::Container => "container",
   407→            Self::Hybrid => "hybrid",
   408→            Self::Shared => "shared",
   409→        }
   410→    }
   411→}
   412→
   413→impl std::fmt::Display for IsolationStrategy {
   414→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   415→        f.write_str(self.as_str())
   416→    }
   417→}
   418→
   419→impl FromStr for IsolationStrategy {
   420→    type Err = String;
   421→
   422→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   423→        match s {
   424→            "worktree" => Ok(Self::Worktree),
   425→            "container" => Ok(Self::Container),
   426→            "hybrid" => Ok(Self::Hybrid),
   427→            "shared" => Ok(Self::Shared),
   428→            _ => Err(format!("Invalid isolation strategy: {}", s)),
   429→        }
   430→    }
   431→}

   593→/// How the tasks within an [`AgentTeam`] are scheduled relative to each other.

   726→/// A team of agents assembled to execute a single [`PipelineRun`].
   727→///
   728→/// Created by the planner after it analyses the issue and decomposes it into
   729→/// discrete tasks. The team record captures the high-level plan and the chosen
   730→/// execution and isolation strategies. Individual work items are stored as
   731→/// [`AgentTask`] records linked to this team.
   732→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   733→pub struct AgentTeam {
   734→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   735→    pub id: i64,
   736→    /// Foreign key to the [`PipelineRun`] this team is executing.
   737→    pub run_id: i64,
   738→    /// How tasks within the team are scheduled relative to each other.
   739→    pub strategy: ExecutionStrategy,
   740→    /// How each task is isolated from the others on the filesystem.
   741→    pub isolation: IsolationStrategy,
   742→    /// Human-readable summary of the plan produced by the planner agent.
   743→    pub plan_summary: String,
   744→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when the team was created.
   745→    pub created_at: String,
   746→}
   747→
   748→/// An individual task within an [`AgentTeam`], assigned to a specific agent role.
   749→///
   750→/// Tasks represent atomic units of work (e.g. "implement the login handler",
   751→/// "write tests for the login handler"). They are organized into numbered waves
   752→/// so that dependency ordering can be enforced: all tasks in wave N complete
   753→/// before wave N+1 starts.
   754→///
   755→/// Depending on the [`IsolationStrategy`], each task may run in its own `git worktree`
   756→/// (tracked by `worktree_path`) or a container (tracked by `container_id`).
   757→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   758→pub struct AgentTask {
   759→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   760→    pub id: i64,
   761→    /// Foreign key to the [`AgentTeam`] this task belongs to.
   762→    pub team_id: i64,
   763→    /// Short name describing what this specific task does (e.g. `"Implement auth middleware"`).
   764→    pub name: String,
   765→    /// Full instructions passed to the agent as its prompt for this task.
   766→    pub description: String,
   767→    /// The role played by the agent executing this task.
   768→    pub agent_role: AgentRole,
   769→    /// Wave number for dependency-ordered execution. Tasks in the same wave can run in parallel.
   770→    pub wave: i32,
   771→    /// IDs of other [`AgentTask`]s that must complete before this task can start.
   772→    pub depends_on: Vec<i64>,
   773→    /// Current lifecycle state of this task.
   774→    pub status: AgentTaskStatus,
   775→    /// Isolation mode used for this task's filesystem environment.
   776→    pub isolation_type: IsolationStrategy,
   777→    /// Filesystem path to the git worktree created for this task, if using worktree isolation.
   778→    pub worktree_path: Option<String>,
   779→    /// Docker container ID for this task, if using container isolation.
   780→    pub container_id: Option<String>,
   781→    /// Git branch name used by this task (typically `forge/issue-<id>-<role>`).
   782→    pub branch_name: Option<String>,
   783→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when this task began executing.
   784→    pub started_at: Option<String>,
   785→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when this task reached a terminal state.
   786→    pub completed_at: Option<String>,
   787→    /// Error message captured when the task transitions to `Failed`.
   788→    pub error: Option<String>,
   789→}
   790→
   791→/// A real-time event emitted by an agent during task execution.
   792→///
   793→/// Events are written to the database as they occur and broadcast to connected
   794→/// WebSocket clients so the Factory UI can display a live log of what each agent
   795→/// is doing. The `metadata` field carries structured data for `Signal` events
   796→/// (e.g. the parsed `<progress>` or `<blocker>` payload).
   797→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   798→pub struct AgentEvent {
   799→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   800→    pub id: i64,
   801→    /// Foreign key to the [`AgentTask`] that emitted this event.
   802→    pub task_id: i64,
   803→    /// Classifies the nature of the event for filtering and display.
   804→    pub event_type: AgentEventType,
   805→    /// The raw textual content of the event.
   806→    pub content: String,
   807→    /// Optional structured JSON payload, present for `Signal` events.
   808→    pub metadata: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   809→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was recorded.
   810→    pub created_at: String,
   811→}
   812→
   813→/// Aggregated view of an [`AgentTeam`] together with all of its tasks.
   814→///
   815→/// Returned by the `GET /api/runs/:id/team` endpoint and used by the agent
   816→/// dashboard panel in the Factory UI to render the full team status at a glance.
   817→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   818→pub struct AgentTeamDetail {
   819→    /// The agent team metadata and configuration.
   820→    pub team: AgentTeam,
   821→    /// All tasks belonging to the team, ordered by wave then task ID.
   822→    pub tasks: Vec<AgentTask>,
   823→}
   824→
   825→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   826→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   827→pub enum FileAction {
   828→    Created,
   829→    Modified,
   830→    Deleted,
   831→}

86→    fn run_migrations(&self) -> Result<()> {
    87→        self.conn
    88→            .execute_batch(
    89→                "
    90→                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
    91→                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    92→                    name TEXT NOT NULL,
    93→                    path TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    94→                    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    95→                );
    96→
    97→                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues (
    98→                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    99→                    project_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   100→                    title TEXT NOT NULL,
   101→                    description TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
   102→                    column_name TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'backlog',
   103→                    position INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
   104→                    priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
   105→                    labels TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
   106→                    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   107→                    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   108→                );
   109→
   110→                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pipeline_runs (
   111→                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   112→                    issue_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   113→                    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued',
   114→                    phase_count INTEGER,
   115→                    current_phase INTEGER,
   116→                    iteration INTEGER,
   117→                    summary TEXT,
   118→                    error TEXT,
   119→                    branch_name TEXT,
   120→                    pr_url TEXT,
   121→                    started_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   122→                    completed_at TEXT
   123→                );
   124→
   125→                CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pipeline_phases (
   126→                    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   127→                    run_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES pipeline_runs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   128→                    phase_number TEXT NOT NULL,
   129→                    phase_name TEXT NOT NULL,
   130→                    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   131→                    iteration INTEGER,
   132→                    budget INTEGER,
   133→                    started_at TEXT,
   134→                    completed_at TEXT,
   135→                    error TEXT,
   136→                    UNIQUE(run_id, phase_number)
   137→                );
   138→
   139→                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_project ON issues(project_id);
   140→                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_column ON issues(project_id, column_name);
   141→                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pipeline_runs_issue ON pipeline_runs(issue_id);
   142→                CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_pipeline_phases_run ON pipeline_phases(run_id);
   143→                ",
   144→            )
   145→            .context("Failed to create tables")?;
   146→
   147→        // Additive migrations (columns are nullable, safe to re-run).
   148→        // We only ignore "duplicate column" errors — any other error is propagated.
   149→        match self
   150→            .conn
   151→            .execute("ALTER TABLE projects ADD COLUMN github_repo TEXT", [])
   152→        {
   153→            Ok(_) => {}
   154→            Err(e) if e.to_string().contains("duplicate column") => {}
   155→            Err(e) => return Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Failed to add github_repo column: {}", e)),
   156→        }
   157→        match self.conn.execute(
   158→            "ALTER TABLE issues ADD COLUMN github_issue_number INTEGER",
   159→            [],
   160→        ) {
   161→            Ok(_) => {}
   162→            Err(e) if e.to_string().contains("duplicate column") => {}
   163→            Err(e) => {
   164→                return Err(anyhow::anyhow!(
   165→                    "Failed to add github_issue_number column: {}",
   166→                    e
   167→                ));
   168→            }
   169→        }
   170→        self.conn
   171→            .execute_batch(

   430→                    column_name: row.get(4)?,
   431→                    position: row.get(5)?,

   435→                    created_at: row.get(9)?,
   436→                    updated_at: row.get(10)?,
   437→                })
   438→            })
   439→            .context("Failed to query issues")?;
   440→        let mut issues = Vec::new();
   441→        for row in rows {

   443→            issues.push(r.into_issue()?);
   444→        }

   447→
   448→    pub fn get_issue(&self, id: i64) -> Result<Option<Issue>> {
   449→        let mut stmt = self
   450→            .conn
   451→            .prepare(
   452→                "SELECT id, project_id, title, description, column_name, position, priority, labels, github_issue_number, created_at, updated_at
   453→                 FROM issues WHERE id = ?1",
   454→            )
   455→            .context("Failed to prepare get_issue")?;
   456→        let mut rows = stmt
   457→            .query_map(params![id], |row| {
   458→                Ok(IssueRow {
   459→                    id: row.get(0)?,
   460→                    project_id: row.get(1)?,
   461→                    title: row.get(2)?,
   462→                    description: row.get(3)?,
   463→                    column_name: row.get(4)?,
   464→                    position: row.get(5)?,
   465→                    priority: row.get(6)?,
   466→                    labels: row.get(7)?,
   467→                    github_issue_number: row.get(8)?,
   468→                    created_at: row.get(9)?,
   469→                    updated_at: row.get(10)?,
   470→                })
   471→            })
   472→            .context("Failed to query issue")?;
   473→        match rows.next() {
   474→            Some(row) => {
   475→                let r = row.context("Failed to read issue row")?;
   476→                Ok(Some(r.into_issue()?))
   477→            }
   478→            None => Ok(None),
   479→        }
   480→    }
   481→
   482→    pub fn update_issue(
   483→        &self,
   484→        id: i64,
   485→        title: Option<&str>,
   486→        description: Option<&str>,
   487→        priority: Option<&str>,
   488→        labels: Option<&str>,
   489→    ) -> Result<Issue> {
   490→        // Validate priority before touching the DB.
   491→        if let Some(p) = priority {
   492→            Priority::from_str(p)
   493→                .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(e))
   494→                .context("Invalid priority value")?;
   495→        }
   496→
   497→        // Use unchecked_transaction so all updates are atomic.
   498→        // Safety: DbHandle's Mutex already guarantees single-threaded access.
   499→        let tx = self
   500→            .conn
   501→            .unchecked_transaction()
   502→            .context("Failed to begin transaction")?;
   503→
   504→        if let Some(t) = title {
   505→            tx.execute(
   506→                "UPDATE issues SET title = ?1, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?2",
   507→                params![t, id],
   508→            )
   509→            .context("Failed to update issue title")?;
   510→        }
   511→        if let Some(d) = description {
   512→            tx.execute(
   513→                "UPDATE issues SET description = ?1, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?2",
   514→                params![d, id],
   515→            )
   516→            .context("Failed to update issue description")?;
   517→        }
   518→        if let Some(p) = priority {
   519→            tx.execute(
   520→                "UPDATE issues SET priority = ?1, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?2",
   521→                params![p, id],
   522→            )
   523→            .context("Failed to update issue priority")?;
   524→        }
   525→        if let Some(l) = labels {
   526→            tx.execute(
   527→                "UPDATE issues SET labels = ?1, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?2",
   528→                params![l, id],
   529→            )
   530→            .context("Failed to update issue labels")?;
   531→        }
   532→
   533→        tx.commit().context("Failed to commit issue update")?;
   534→        self.get_issue(id)?.context("Issue not found after update")
   535→    }
   536→
   537→    pub fn move_issue(&self, id: i64, column: &IssueColumn, position: i32) -> Result<Issue> {
   538→        self.conn
   539→            .execute(
   540→                "UPDATE issues SET column_name = ?1, position = ?2, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?3",
   541→                params![column.as_str(), position, id],
   542→            )
   543→            .context("Failed to move issue")?;
   544→        self.get_issue(id)?.context("Issue not found after move")
   545→    }
   546→
   547→    pub fn delete_issue(&self, id: i64) -> Result<bool> {
   548→        let count = self

   551→            .context("Failed to delete issue")?;
   552→        Ok(count > 0)
   553→    }

   584→    }

   332→    pub fn delete_project(&self, id: i64) -> Result<bool> {
   333→        // Manually clean up agent tables that lack ON DELETE CASCADE.
   334→        // Chain: project -> issues -> pipeline_runs -> agent_teams -> agent_tasks -> agent_events
   335→        self.conn
   336→            .execute(
   337→                "DELETE FROM agent_events WHERE task_id IN (
   338→                    SELECT t.id FROM agent_tasks t
   339→                    JOIN agent_teams at ON t.team_id = at.id
   340→                    JOIN pipeline_runs r ON at.run_id = r.id
   341→                    JOIN issues i ON r.issue_id = i.id
   342→                    WHERE i.project_id = ?1
   343→                )",
   344→                params![id],
   345→            )
   346→            .context("Failed to delete agent events for project")?;
   347→        self.conn
   348→            .execute(
   349→                "DELETE FROM agent_tasks WHERE team_id IN (
   350→                    SELECT at.id FROM agent_teams at
   351→                    JOIN pipeline_runs r ON at.run_id = r.id
   352→                    JOIN issues i ON r.issue_id = i.id
   353→                    WHERE i.project_id = ?1
   354→                )",
   355→                params![id],
   356→            )
   357→            .context("Failed to delete agent tasks for project")?;
   358→        // Null out the back-reference from pipeline_runs.team_id before removing teams
   359→        self.conn
   360→            .execute(
   361→                "UPDATE pipeline_runs SET team_id = NULL WHERE issue_id IN (
   362→                    SELECT i.id FROM issues i WHERE i.project_id = ?1
   363→                )",
   364→                params![id],
   365→            )
   366→            .context("Failed to clear team_id on pipeline runs")?;
   367→        self.conn
   368→            .execute(
   369→                "DELETE FROM agent_teams WHERE run_id IN (
   370→                    SELECT r.id FROM pipeline_runs r
   371→                    JOIN issues i ON r.issue_id = i.id
   372→                    WHERE i.project_id = ?1
   373→                )",
   374→                params![id],
   375→            )
   376→            .context("Failed to delete agent teams for project")?;
   377→
   378→        let count = self
   379→            .conn
   380→            .execute("DELETE FROM projects WHERE id = ?1", params![id])
   381→            .context("Failed to delete project")?;
   382→        Ok(count > 0)
   383→    }

    25→/// The column an issue currently occupies in the Kanban board.
    26→///
    27→/// Columns define the workflow lifecycle of an issue. Moving an issue to
    28→/// `InProgress` triggers automatic pipeline execution via [`crate::factory::pipeline::PipelineRunner`].
    29→/// The typical progression is:
    30→///
    31→/// `Backlog` → `Ready` → `InProgress` → `InReview` → `Done`

    33→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
    34→pub enum IssueColumn {
    35→    /// Work that has been captured but is not yet scheduled for development.

    41→    /// Moving into this column triggers a [`PipelineRun`] to be created and
    42→    /// execution to begin via [`crate::factory::pipeline::PipelineRunner`].
    43→    InProgress,

    45→    InReview,

    47→    Done,
    48→}

   154→/// State machine for a [`PipelineRun`].
   155→///
   156→/// Transitions follow the path: `Queued` → `Running` → `Completed` | `Failed` | `Cancelled`.
   157→/// Terminal states are identified by [`PipelineStatus::is_terminal`].
   158→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
   159→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   160→pub enum PipelineStatus {
   161→    /// The run has been created and is waiting for an executor slot.
   162→    Queued,
   163→    /// The pipeline is actively executing phases via `forge`.
   164→    Running,
   165→    /// All phases finished successfully. Terminal state.
   166→    Completed,
   167→    /// The pipeline encountered an unrecoverable error. Terminal state.
   168→    Failed,
   169→    /// The run was explicitly cancelled before completion. Terminal state.
   170→    Cancelled,
   171→}
   172→
   173→impl PipelineStatus {
   174→    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   175→        match self {
   176→            Self::Queued => "queued",
   177→            Self::Running => "running",
   178→            Self::Completed => "completed",
   179→            Self::Failed => "failed",
   180→            Self::Cancelled => "cancelled",
   181→        }
   182→    }
   183→}
   184→
   185→impl FromStr for PipelineStatus {
   186→    type Err = String;
   187→
   188→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   189→        match s {
   190→            "queued" => Ok(Self::Queued),
   191→            "running" => Ok(Self::Running),
   192→            "completed" => Ok(Self::Completed),
   193→            "failed" => Ok(Self::Failed),
   194→            "cancelled" => Ok(Self::Cancelled),
   195→            _ => Err(format!("Invalid pipeline status: {}", s)),
   196→        }
   197→    }
   198→}
   199→
   200→impl PipelineStatus {
   201→    /// Returns true for terminal states that indicate the pipeline is no longer running.
   202→    pub fn is_terminal(&self) -> bool {
   203→        matches!(self, Self::Completed | Self::Failed | Self::Cancelled)
   204→    }
   205→}

   291→/// Each phase has an iteration budget; the pipeline retries the phase until the agent
   292→/// emits `<promise>DONE</promise>` or the budget is exhausted.
   293→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   294→pub struct PipelinePhase {
   295→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   296→    pub id: i64,
   297→    /// Foreign key to the owning [`PipelineRun`].
   298→    pub run_id: i64,
   299→    /// Hierarchical phase identifier from the spec (e.g. `"1"`, `"2.1"`).
   300→    pub phase_number: String,
   301→    /// Human-readable name of the phase (e.g. `"Implement feature"`).
   302→    pub phase_name: String,
   303→    /// Current execution state of this phase.
   304→    pub status: PhaseStatus,
   305→    /// Current iteration count within this phase (incremented on each retry).
   306→    pub iteration: Option<i32>,
   307→    /// Maximum number of iterations allowed for this phase before it is marked failed.
   308→    pub budget: Option<i32>,
   309→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when this phase began executing.
   310→    pub started_at: Option<String>,
   311→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when this phase reached a terminal state.
   312→    pub completed_at: Option<String>,
   313→    /// Error message if the phase failed.
   314→    pub error: Option<String>,
   315→}

   378→// Agent team execution models
   379→
   380→/// Isolation strategy for agent task execution.
   381→///
   382→/// Controls how each [`AgentTask`] within a team is isolated from others to
   383→/// prevent file-system conflicts when multiple agents work in parallel.
   384→///
   385→/// Currently only `Worktree` and `Shared` are fully implemented.
   386→/// `Container` and `Hybrid` are reserved for future use.
   387→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   388→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   389→pub enum IsolationStrategy {
   390→    /// Each agent task operates in a dedicated `git worktree` branched from `main`.
   391→    /// Results are merged back after each wave completes.
   392→    Worktree,
   393→    /// Each agent task runs in an isolated Docker container. Reserved for future use.
   394→    Container,
   395→    /// Combines container and worktree isolation. Reserved for future use.
   396→    Hybrid,
   397→    /// All agent tasks share the same working directory. Suitable for sequential strategies
   398→    /// or when tasks are guaranteed to touch non-overlapping files.
   399→    Shared,
   400→}
   401→
   402→impl IsolationStrategy {
   403→    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   404→        match self {
   405→            Self::Worktree => "worktree",
   406→            Self::Container => "container",
   407→            Self::Hybrid => "hybrid",
   408→            Self::Shared => "shared",
   409→        }
   410→    }
   411→}
   412→
   413→impl std::fmt::Display for IsolationStrategy {
   414→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   415→        f.write_str(self.as_str())
   416→    }
   417→}
   418→
   419→impl FromStr for IsolationStrategy {
   420→    type Err = String;
   421→
   422→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   423→        match s {
   424→            "worktree" => Ok(Self::Worktree),
   425→            "container" => Ok(Self::Container),
   426→            "hybrid" => Ok(Self::Hybrid),
   427→            "shared" => Ok(Self::Shared),
   428→            _ => Err(format!("Invalid isolation strategy: {}", s)),
   429→        }
   430→    }
   431→}

   433→/// Roles that agents can assume during team execution of a pipeline run.
   434→///
   435→/// The planner assigns `Coder`, `Tester`, and `Reviewer` tasks when decomposing
   436→/// an issue. `Planner`, `BrowserVerifier`, and `TestVerifier` are intended for

   447→    /// Reviews code changes for correctness, style, and adherence to requirements.
   448→    Reviewer,
   449→    /// Verifies the implementation via browser automation. Reserved for future use.

   467→

   490→/// Lifecycle state of an individual [`AgentTask`].
   491→///
   492→/// Transitions follow the path: `Pending` → `Running` → `Completed` | `Failed` | `Cancelled`.
   493→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   494→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   495→pub enum AgentTaskStatus {
   496→    /// Task has been created but its wave has not started executing yet.
   497→    Pending,
   498→    /// Task is actively running its assigned agent process.
   499→    Running,
   500→    /// Task finished successfully. Terminal state.
   501→    Completed,
   502→    /// Task encountered an unrecoverable error. Terminal state.
   503→    Failed,
   504→    /// Task was cancelled, typically because a sibling task in the same wave failed. Terminal state.
   505→    Cancelled,
   506→}
   507→
   508→impl AgentTaskStatus {
   509→    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   510→        match self {
   511→            Self::Pending => "pending",
   512→            Self::Running => "running",
   513→            Self::Completed => "completed",
   514→            Self::Failed => "failed",
   515→            Self::Cancelled => "cancelled",
   516→        }
   517→    }
   518→}
   519→
   520→impl std::fmt::Display for AgentTaskStatus {
   521→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
   522→        f.write_str(self.as_str())
   523→    }
   524→}
   525→
   526→impl FromStr for AgentTaskStatus {
   527→    type Err = String;
   528→
   529→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   530→        match s {
   531→            "pending" => Ok(Self::Pending),
   532→            "running" => Ok(Self::Running),
   533→            "completed" => Ok(Self::Completed),
   534→            "failed" => Ok(Self::Failed),
   535→            "cancelled" => Ok(Self::Cancelled),
   536→            _ => Err(format!("Invalid agent task status: {}", s)),
   537→        }
   538→    }
   539→}

   593→/// How the tasks within an [`AgentTeam`] are scheduled relative to each other.
   594→///
   595→/// The strategy is chosen by the planner based on the nature of the issue and
   596→/// stored on the [`AgentTeam`]. It determines how the executor launches tasks
   597→/// and whether inter-task dependencies are enforced.
   598→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]

   600→pub enum ExecutionStrategy {
   601→    /// All tasks are started simultaneously with no ordering constraints.
   602→    Parallel,
   603→    /// Tasks are executed one at a time in dependency order.
   604→    Sequential,
   605→    /// Tasks are executed in waves: all tasks in wave N complete before wave N+1 begins.
   606→    /// This is the primary strategy for Coder → Tester → Reviewer pipelines.
   607→    WavePipeline,
   608→    /// The executor dynamically adjusts scheduling based on runtime signals. Reserved for future use.
   609→    Adaptive,
   610→}

   613→    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &'static str {
   614→        match self {
   615→            Self::Parallel => "parallel",

   617→            Self::WavePipeline => "wave_pipeline",
   618→            Self::Adaptive => "adaptive",
   619→        }
   620→    }
   621→}
   622→
   623→impl std::fmt::Display for ExecutionStrategy {
   624→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {

   627→}

   630→    type Err = String;

   632→    fn from_str(s: &str) -> Result<Self, Self::Err> {
   633→        match s {
   634→            "parallel" => Ok(Self::Parallel),
   635→            "sequential" => Ok(Self::Sequential),
   636→            "wave_pipeline" => Ok(Self::WavePipeline),
   637→            "adaptive" => Ok(Self::Adaptive),

   640→    }
   641→}

   726→/// A team of agents assembled to execute a single [`PipelineRun`].
   727→///
   728→/// Created by the planner after it analyses the issue and decomposes it into
   729→/// discrete tasks. The team record captures the high-level plan and the chosen
   730→/// execution and isolation strategies. Individual work items are stored as
   731→/// [`AgentTask`] records linked to this team.
   732→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   733→pub struct AgentTeam {
   734→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   735→    pub id: i64,
   736→    /// Foreign key to the [`PipelineRun`] this team is executing.
   737→    pub run_id: i64,
   738→    /// How tasks within the team are scheduled relative to each other.
   739→    pub strategy: ExecutionStrategy,
   740→    /// How each task is isolated from the others on the filesystem.
   741→    pub isolation: IsolationStrategy,
   742→    /// Human-readable summary of the plan produced by the planner agent.
   743→    pub plan_summary: String,
   744→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when the team was created.
   745→    pub created_at: String,
   746→}
   747→
   748→/// An individual task within an [`AgentTeam`], assigned to a specific agent role.
   749→///
   750→/// Tasks represent atomic units of work (e.g. "implement the login handler",
   751→/// "write tests for the login handler"). They are organized into numbered waves
   752→/// so that dependency ordering can be enforced: all tasks in wave N complete
   753→/// before wave N+1 starts.
   754→///
   755→/// Depending on the [`IsolationStrategy`], each task may run in its own `git worktree`
   756→/// (tracked by `worktree_path`) or a container (tracked by `container_id`).
   757→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   758→pub struct AgentTask {
   759→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   760→    pub id: i64,
   761→    /// Foreign key to the [`AgentTeam`] this task belongs to.
   762→    pub team_id: i64,
   763→    /// Short name describing what this specific task does (e.g. `"Implement auth middleware"`).
   764→    pub name: String,
   765→    /// Full instructions passed to the agent as its prompt for this task.
   766→    pub description: String,
   767→    /// The role played by the agent executing this task.
   768→    pub agent_role: AgentRole,
   769→    /// Wave number for dependency-ordered execution. Tasks in the same wave can run in parallel.
   770→    pub wave: i32,
   771→    /// IDs of other [`AgentTask`]s that must complete before this task can start.
   772→    pub depends_on: Vec<i64>,
   773→    /// Current lifecycle state of this task.
   774→    pub status: AgentTaskStatus,
   775→    /// Isolation mode used for this task's filesystem environment.
   776→    pub isolation_type: IsolationStrategy,
   777→    /// Filesystem path to the git worktree created for this task, if using worktree isolation.
   778→    pub worktree_path: Option<String>,
   779→    /// Docker container ID for this task, if using container isolation.
   780→    pub container_id: Option<String>,
   781→    /// Git branch name used by this task (typically `forge/issue-<id>-<role>`).
   782→    pub branch_name: Option<String>,
   783→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when this task began executing.
   784→    pub started_at: Option<String>,
   785→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when this task reached a terminal state.
   786→    pub completed_at: Option<String>,
   787→    /// Error message captured when the task transitions to `Failed`.
   788→    pub error: Option<String>,
   789→}
   790→
   791→/// A real-time event emitted by an agent during task execution.
   792→///
   793→/// Events are written to the database as they occur and broadcast to connected
   794→/// WebSocket clients so the Factory UI can display a live log of what each agent
   795→/// is doing. The `metadata` field carries structured data for `Signal` events
   796→/// (e.g. the parsed `<progress>` or `<blocker>` payload).
   797→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   798→pub struct AgentEvent {
   799→    /// Unique integer identifier assigned by the database.
   800→    pub id: i64,
   801→    /// Foreign key to the [`AgentTask`] that emitted this event.
   802→    pub task_id: i64,
   803→    /// Classifies the nature of the event for filtering and display.
   804→    pub event_type: AgentEventType,
   805→    /// The raw textual content of the event.
   806→    pub content: String,
   807→    /// Optional structured JSON payload, present for `Signal` events.
   808→    pub metadata: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   809→    /// ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event was recorded.
   810→    pub created_at: String,
   811→}
   812→
   813→/// Aggregated view of an [`AgentTeam`] together with all of its tasks.
   814→///
   815→/// Returned by the `GET /api/runs/:id/team` endpoint and used by the agent
   816→/// dashboard panel in the Factory UI to render the full team status at a glance.
   817→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   818→pub struct AgentTeamDetail {
   819→    /// The agent team metadata and configuration.
   820→    pub team: AgentTeam,
   821→    /// All tasks belonging to the team, ordered by wave then task ID.
   822→    pub tasks: Vec<AgentTask>,
   823→}

   826→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]

   833→#[cfg(test)]
   834→mod tests {
   835→    use super::*;
   836→
   837→    #[test]
   838→    fn test_isolation_strategy_roundtrip() {
   839→        for s in &["worktree", "container", "hybrid", "shared"] {
   840→            let parsed: IsolationStrategy = s.parse().unwrap();
   841→            assert_eq!(parsed.as_str(), *s);
   842→        }
   843→        assert!("invalid".parse::<IsolationStrategy>().is_err());
   844→    }
   845→
   846→    #[test]
   847→    fn test_agent_role_roundtrip() {
   848→        for s in &[
   849→            "planner",
   850→            "coder",
   851→            "tester",
   852→            "reviewer",
   853→            "browser_verifier",
   854→            "test_verifier",
   855→        ] {
   856→            let parsed: AgentRole = s.parse().unwrap();
   857→            assert_eq!(parsed.as_str(), *s);
   858→        }
   859→        assert!("invalid".parse::<AgentRole>().is_err());
   860→    }
   861→

   660→    pub fn get_issue_detail(&self, id: i64) -> Result<Option<IssueDetail>> {
   661→        let issue = match self.get_issue(id)? {
   662→            Some(i) => i,
   663→            None => return Ok(None),
   664→        };
   665→
   666→        let mut stmt = self
   667→            .conn
   668→            .prepare(
   669→                "SELECT id, issue_id, status, phase_count, current_phase, iteration, summary, error, branch_name, pr_url, team_id, has_team, started_at, completed_at
   670→                 FROM pipeline_runs WHERE issue_id = ?1 ORDER BY id",
   671→            )
   672→            .context("Failed to prepare get_issue_detail runs")?;
   673→        let rows = stmt
   674→            .query_map(params![id], |row| {
   675→                Ok(PipelineRunRow {
   676→                    id: row.get(0)?,
   677→                    issue_id: row.get(1)?,
   678→                    status: row.get(2)?,
   679→                    phase_count: row.get(3)?,
   680→                    current_phase: row.get(4)?,
   681→                    iteration: row.get(5)?,
   682→                    summary: row.get(6)?,
   683→                    error: row.get(7)?,
   684→                    branch_name: row.get(8)?,
   685→                    pr_url: row.get(9)?,
   686→                    team_id: row.get(10)?,
   687→                    has_team: row.get(11)?,
   688→                    started_at: row.get(12)?,
   689→                    completed_at: row.get(13)?,
   690→                })
   691→            })
   692→            .context("Failed to query pipeline runs")?;
   693→        let mut runs = Vec::new();
   694→        for row in rows {
   695→            let r = row.context("Failed to read pipeline_run row")?;
   696→            let run = r.into_pipeline_run()?;
   697→            let phases = self.get_pipeline_phases(run.id)?;
   698→            runs.push(PipelineRunDetail { run, phases });
   699→        }
   700→
   701→        Ok(Some(IssueDetail { issue, runs }))
   702→    }

   706→    pub fn create_pipeline_run(&self, issue_id: i64) -> Result<PipelineRun> {
   707→        self.conn
   708→            .execute(
   709→                "INSERT INTO pipeline_runs (issue_id) VALUES (?1)",
   710→                params![issue_id],
   711→            )
   712→            .context("Failed to insert pipeline run")?;
   713→        let id = self.conn.last_insert_rowid();
   714→        self.get_pipeline_run(id)?
   715→            .context("Pipeline run not found after insert")
   716→    }

   718→    pub fn update_pipeline_run(
   719→        &self,
   720→        id: i64,
   721→        status: &PipelineStatus,
   722→        summary: Option<&str>,
   723→        error: Option<&str>,
   724→    ) -> Result<PipelineRun> {
   725→        let is_terminal = status.is_terminal();
   726→
   727→        if is_terminal {
   728→            self.conn
   729→                .execute(
   730→                    "UPDATE pipeline_runs SET status = ?1, summary = ?2, error = ?3, completed_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?4",
   731→                    params![status.as_str(), summary, error, id],
   732→                )
   733→                .context("Failed to update pipeline run")?;
   734→        } else {
   735→            self.conn
   736→                .execute(
   737→                    "UPDATE pipeline_runs SET status = ?1, summary = ?2, error = ?3 WHERE id = ?4",
   738→                    params![status.as_str(), summary, error, id],
   739→                )
   740→                .context("Failed to update pipeline run")?;
   741→        }
   742→
   743→        self.get_pipeline_run(id)?
   744→            .context("Pipeline run not found after update")
   745→    }
   746→
   747→    pub fn get_pipeline_run(&self, id: i64) -> Result<Option<PipelineRun>> {
   748→        let mut stmt = self
   749→            .conn
   750→            .prepare(
   751→                "SELECT id, issue_id, status, phase_count, current_phase, iteration, summary, error, branch_name, pr_url, team_id, has_team, started_at, completed_at
   752→                 FROM pipeline_runs WHERE id = ?1",
   753→            )
   754→            .context("Failed to prepare get_pipeline_run")?;
   755→        let mut rows = stmt
   756→            .query_map(params![id], |row| {
   757→                Ok(PipelineRunRow {
   758→                    id: row.get(0)?,
   759→                    issue_id: row.get(1)?,
   760→                    status: row.get(2)?,
   761→                    phase_count: row.get(3)?,
   762→                    current_phase: row.get(4)?,
   763→                    iteration: row.get(5)?,
   764→                    summary: row.get(6)?,
   765→                    error: row.get(7)?,
   766→                    branch_name: row.get(8)?,
   767→                    pr_url: row.get(9)?,
   768→                    team_id: row.get(10)?,
   769→                    has_team: row.get(11)?,
   770→                    started_at: row.get(12)?,
   771→                    completed_at: row.get(13)?,
   772→                })
   773→            })
   774→            .context("Failed to query pipeline run")?;
   775→        match rows.next() {
   776→            Some(row) => {
   777→                let r = row.context("Failed to read pipeline_run row")?;
   778→                Ok(Some(r.into_pipeline_run()?))
   779→            }
   780→            None => Ok(None),
   781→        }
   782→    }

   788→    /// Mark all orphaned "running" or "queued" pipeline runs as failed.
   789→    /// Called on server startup to clean up runs from a previous server instance
   790→    /// whose subprocesses no longer exist.
   791→    pub fn recover_orphaned_runs(&self) -> Result<usize> {
   792→        let count = self.conn.execute(
   793→            "UPDATE pipeline_runs SET status = 'failed', error = 'Server restarted — pipeline process was lost', completed_at = datetime('now') WHERE status IN ('running', 'queued')",
   794→            [],
   795→        ).context("Failed to recover orphaned pipeline runs")?;
   796→
   797→        // Also move any in_progress issues (whose runs are now failed) back to ready
   798→        if count > 0 {
   799→            self.conn.execute(
   800→                "UPDATE issues SET column_name = 'ready' WHERE column_name = 'in_progress' AND id IN (SELECT issue_id FROM pipeline_runs WHERE error = 'Server restarted — pipeline process was lost')",
   801→                [],
   802→            ).context("Failed to move orphaned issues back to ready")?;
   803→        }
   804→
   805→        Ok(count)
   806→    }

   850→    // ── Pipeline Phase CRUD ─────────────────────────────────────────
   851→
   852→    /// Create or update a pipeline phase record.
   853→    ///
   854→    /// Accepts a `&str` status for backward compatibility with existing callers.
   855→    /// The status string is stored as-is in the database and parsed into
   856→    /// `PhaseStatus` when reading back via `get_pipeline_phases`.
   857→    pub fn upsert_pipeline_phase(
   858→        &self,
   859→        run_id: i64,
   860→        phase_number: &str,
   861→        phase_name: &str,
   862→        status: &str,
   863→        iteration: Option<i32>,
   864→        budget: Option<i32>,
   865→    ) -> Result<()> {
   866→        self.conn
   867→            .execute(
   868→                "INSERT INTO pipeline_phases (run_id, phase_number, phase_name, status, iteration, budget, started_at)
   869→                 VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, datetime('now'))
   870→                 ON CONFLICT(run_id, phase_number) DO UPDATE SET
   871→                    status = ?4,
   872→                    iteration = COALESCE(?5, pipeline_phases.iteration),
   873→                    budget = COALESCE(?6, pipeline_phases.budget),
   874→                    completed_at = CASE WHEN ?4 IN ('completed', 'failed') THEN datetime('now') ELSE pipeline_phases.completed_at END,
   875→                    error = NULL",
   876→                params![run_id, phase_number, phase_name, status, iteration, budget],
   877→            )
   878→            .context("Failed to upsert pipeline phase")?;
   879→        Ok(())
   880→    }
   881→
   882→    /// Create or update a pipeline phase record using a typed `PhaseStatus`.
   883→    pub fn upsert_pipeline_phase_typed(
   884→        &self,
   885→        run_id: i64,
   886→        phase_number: &str,
   887→        phase_name: &str,
   888→        status: &PhaseStatus,
   889→        iteration: Option<i32>,
   890→        budget: Option<i32>,
   891→    ) -> Result<()> {
   892→        self.upsert_pipeline_phase(
   893→            run_id,
   894→            phase_number,
   895→            phase_name,
   896→            &status.to_string(),
   897→            iteration,
   898→            budget,
   899→        )
   900→    }
   901→
   902→    /// Get all phases for a pipeline run.
   903→    pub fn get_pipeline_phases(&self, run_id: i64) -> Result<Vec<PipelinePhase>> {
   904→        let mut stmt = self
   905→            .conn
   906→            .prepare(
   907→                "SELECT id, run_id, phase_number, phase_name, status, iteration, budget, started_at, completed_at, error
   908→                 FROM pipeline_phases WHERE run_id = ?1 ORDER BY phase_number ASC",
   909→            )
   910→            .context("Failed to prepare get_pipeline_phases")?;
   911→        let rows = stmt
   912→            .query_map(params![run_id], |row| {
   913→                let status_str: String = row.get(4)?;
   914→                let status = status_str
   915→                    .parse::<PhaseStatus>()
   916→                    .unwrap_or(PhaseStatus::Pending);
   917→                Ok(PipelinePhase {
   918→                    id: row.get(0)?,
   919→                    run_id: row.get(1)?,
   920→                    phase_number: row.get(2)?,
   921→                    phase_name: row.get(3)?,
   922→                    status,
   923→                    iteration: row.get(5)?,
   924→                    budget: row.get(6)?,
   925→                    started_at: row.get(7)?,
   926→                    completed_at: row.get(8)?,
   927→                    error: row.get(9)?,
   928→                })
   929→            })
   930→            .context("Failed to query pipeline phases")?;
   931→
   932→        let mut phases = Vec::new();
   933→        for row in rows {
   934→            phases.push(row.context("Failed to read pipeline phase row")?);
   935→        }
   936→        Ok(phases)
   937→    }

   941→    pub fn create_agent_team(
   942→        &self,
   943→        run_id: i64,
   944→        strategy: &ExecutionStrategy,
   945→        isolation: &IsolationStrategy,
   946→        plan_summary: &str,
   947→    ) -> Result<AgentTeam> {
   948→        self.conn.execute(
   949→            "INSERT INTO agent_teams (run_id, strategy, isolation, plan_summary) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4)",
   950→            params![run_id, strategy.as_str(), isolation.as_str(), plan_summary],
   951→        ).context("Failed to insert agent team")?;
   952→        let id = self.conn.last_insert_rowid();
   953→
   954→        // Link team to pipeline run
   955→        self.conn
   956→            .execute(
   957→                "UPDATE pipeline_runs SET team_id = ?1, has_team = 1 WHERE id = ?2",
   958→                params![id, run_id],
   959→            )
   960→            .context("Failed to link agent team to pipeline run")?;
   961→
   962→        Ok(AgentTeam {
   963→            id,
   964→            run_id,
   965→            strategy: strategy.clone(),
   966→            isolation: isolation.clone(),
   967→            plan_summary: plan_summary.to_string(),
   968→            created_at: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
   969→        })
   970→    }

   972→    pub fn get_agent_team(&self, team_id: i64) -> Result<AgentTeam> {

   974→            "SELECT id, run_id, strategy, isolation, plan_summary, created_at FROM agent_teams WHERE id = ?1",
   975→            params![team_id],
   976→            |row| Ok((
   977→                row.get::<_, i64>(0)?,
   978→                row.get::<_, i64>(1)?,
   979→                row.get::<_, String>(2)?,
   980→                row.get::<_, String>(3)?,
   981→                row.get::<_, String>(4)?,
   982→                row.get::<_, String>(5)?,
   983→            )),
   984→        ).context("Agent team not found")?;
   985→        Ok(AgentTeam {
   986→            id,
   987→            run_id,
   988→            strategy: strategy_str
   989→                .parse()
   990→                .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("invalid strategy in database: '{}'", strategy_str))?,
   991→            isolation: isolation_str.parse().map_err(|_| {
   992→                anyhow::anyhow!("invalid isolation in database: '{}'", isolation_str)
   993→            })?,
   994→            plan_summary,
   995→            created_at,
   996→        })

   999→    pub fn get_agent_team_by_run(&self, run_id: i64) -> Result<Option<AgentTeam>> {
  1000→        let mut stmt = self.conn.prepare(
  1001→            "SELECT id, run_id, strategy, isolation, plan_summary, created_at FROM agent_teams WHERE run_id = ?1"
  1002→        ).context("Failed to prepare get_agent_team_by_run")?;
  1003→        let mut rows = stmt.query_map(params![run_id], |row| {
  1004→            Ok((
  1005→                row.get::<_, i64>(0)?,
  1006→                row.get::<_, i64>(1)?,
  1007→                row.get::<_, String>(2)?,
  1008→                row.get::<_, String>(3)?,
  1009→                row.get::<_, String>(4)?,
  1010→                row.get::<_, String>(5)?,
  1011→            ))
  1012→        })?;
  1013→        match rows.next() {
  1014→            Some(row) => {
  1015→                let (id, run_id, strategy_str, isolation_str, plan_summary, created_at) = row?;
  1016→                Ok(Some(AgentTeam {
  1017→                    id,
  1018→                    run_id,
  1019→                    strategy: strategy_str.parse().map_err(|_| {
  1020→                        anyhow::anyhow!("invalid strategy in database: '{}'", strategy_str)
  1021→                    })?,
  1022→                    isolation: isolation_str.parse().map_err(|_| {
  1023→                        anyhow::anyhow!("invalid isolation in database: '{}'", isolation_str)
  1024→                    })?,
  1025→                    plan_summary,
  1026→                    created_at,
  1027→                }))
  1028→            }

  1035→    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
  1036→    pub fn create_agent_task(
  1037→        &self,
  1038→        team_id: i64,
  1039→        name: &str,
  1040→        description: &str,
  1041→        agent_role: &AgentRole,
  1042→        wave: i32,
  1043→        depends_on: &[i64],
  1044→        isolation_type: &IsolationStrategy,
  1045→    ) -> Result<AgentTask> {
  1046→        let depends_json =
  1047→            serde_json::to_string(depends_on).context("Failed to serialize depends_on")?;
  1048→        self.conn.execute(
  1049→            "INSERT INTO agent_tasks (team_id, name, description, agent_role, wave, depends_on, isolation_type) \
  1050→             VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6, ?7)",
  1051→            params![team_id, name, description, agent_role.as_str(), wave, depends_json, isolation_type.as_str()],
  1052→        ).context("Failed to insert agent task")?;
  1053→        let id = self.conn.last_insert_rowid();
  1054→        Ok(AgentTask {
  1055→            id,
  1056→            team_id,
  1057→            name: name.to_string(),
  1058→            description: description.to_string(),
  1059→            agent_role: agent_role.clone(),
  1060→            wave,
  1061→            depends_on: depends_on.to_vec(),
  1062→            status: AgentTaskStatus::Pending,
  1063→            isolation_type: isolation_type.clone(),
  1064→            worktree_path: None,
  1065→            container_id: None,
  1066→            branch_name: None,
  1067→            started_at: None,
  1068→            completed_at: None,
  1069→            error: None,
  1070→        })
  1071→    }

  1073→    pub fn update_agent_task_status(
  1074→        &self,
  1075→        task_id: i64,
  1076→        status: &AgentTaskStatus,
  1077→        error: Option<&str>,
  1078→    ) -> Result<()> {
  1079→        let status_str = status.as_str();
  1080→        match status {
  1081→            AgentTaskStatus::Running => {
  1082→                self.conn.execute(
  1083→                    "UPDATE agent_tasks SET status = ?1, started_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?2",
  1084→                    params![status_str, task_id],
  1085→                ).context("Failed to update agent task status to running")?;
  1086→            }
  1087→            AgentTaskStatus::Completed | AgentTaskStatus::Failed | AgentTaskStatus::Cancelled => {
  1088→                self.conn.execute(
  1089→                    "UPDATE agent_tasks SET status = ?1, error = ?2, completed_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?3",
  1090→                    params![status_str, error, task_id],
  1091→                ).context("Failed to update agent task status to terminal")?;
  1092→            }
  1093→            _ => {
  1094→                self.conn
  1095→                    .execute(
  1096→                        "UPDATE agent_tasks SET status = ?1 WHERE id = ?2",
  1097→                        params![status_str, task_id],
  1098→                    )
  1099→                    .context("Failed to update agent task status")?;

1100→            }
  1101→        }
  1102→        Ok(())
  1103→    }

  1105→    pub fn update_agent_task_isolation(
  1106→        &self,

  1111→    ) -> Result<()> {

  1114→            params![worktree_path, container_id, branch_name, task_id],
  1115→        ).context("Failed to update agent task isolation")?;
  1116→        Ok(())
  1117→    }

  1119→    pub fn get_agent_tasks(&self, team_id: i64) -> Result<Vec<AgentTask>> {
  1120→        let mut stmt = self.conn.prepare(
  1121→            "SELECT id, team_id, name, description, agent_role, wave, depends_on, status, \
  1122→             isolation_type, worktree_path, container_id, branch_name, started_at, completed_at, error \
  1123→             FROM agent_tasks WHERE team_id = ?1 ORDER BY wave, id"
  1124→        ).context("Failed to prepare get_agent_tasks")?;
  1125→        let rows = stmt.query_map(params![team_id], |row| {
  1126→            Ok((
  1127→                row.get::<_, i64>(0)?,
  1128→                row.get::<_, i64>(1)?,
  1129→                row.get::<_, String>(2)?,
  1130→                row.get::<_, String>(3)?,
  1131→                row.get::<_, String>(4)?,
  1132→                row.get::<_, i32>(5)?,
  1133→                row.get::<_, String>(6)?,
  1134→                row.get::<_, String>(7)?,
  1135→                row.get::<_, String>(8)?,
  1136→                row.get::<_, Option<String>>(9)?,
  1137→                row.get::<_, Option<String>>(10)?,
  1138→                row.get::<_, Option<String>>(11)?,
  1139→                row.get::<_, Option<String>>(12)?,
  1140→                row.get::<_, Option<String>>(13)?,
  1141→                row.get::<_, Option<String>>(14)?,
  1142→            ))
  1143→        })?;
  1144→        let mut tasks = Vec::new();
  1145→        for row in rows {
  1146→            let (
  1147→                id,
  1148→                team_id,
  1149→                name,
  1150→                description,
  1151→                agent_role_str,
  1152→                wave,
  1153→                depends_str,
  1154→                status_str,
  1155→                isolation_str,
  1156→                worktree_path,
  1157→                container_id,
  1158→                branch_name,
  1159→                started_at,
  1160→                completed_at,
  1161→                error,
  1162→            ) = row?;
  1163→            let depends_on: Vec<i64> = serde_json::from_str(&depends_str)
  1164→                .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!("corrupt depends_on JSON '{}': {}", depends_str, e))?;
  1165→            tasks.push(AgentTask {
  1166→                id,
  1167→                team_id,
  1168→                name,
  1169→                description,
  1170→                agent_role: agent_role_str.parse().map_err(|_| {
  1171→                    anyhow::anyhow!("invalid agent_role in database: '{}'", agent_role_str)
  1172→                })?,
  1173→                wave,
  1174→                depends_on,
  1175→                status: status_str
  1176→                    .parse()
  1177→                    .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("invalid status in database: '{}'", status_str))?,
  1178→                isolation_type: isolation_str.parse().map_err(|_| {
  1179→                    anyhow::anyhow!("invalid isolation_type in database: '{}'", isolation_str)
  1180→                })?,
  1181→                worktree_path,
  1182→                container_id,
  1183→                branch_name,
  1184→                started_at,
  1185→                completed_at,
  1186→                error,
  1187→            });
  1188→        }
  1189→        Ok(tasks)
  1190→    }

  1210→                    row.get::<_, Option<String>>(9)?,

  1212→                    row.get::<_, Option<String>>(11)?,
  1213→                    row.get::<_, Option<String>>(12)?,
  1214→                    row.get::<_, Option<String>>(13)?,
  1215→                    row.get::<_, Option<String>>(14)?,

  1218→        let depends_on: Vec<i64> = serde_json::from_str(&depends_str)

  1220→        Ok(AgentTask {

  1245→    pub fn get_agent_team_detail(&self, run_id: i64) -> Result<Option<AgentTeamDetail>> {

  1256→    pub fn create_agent_event(
  1257→        &self,
  1258→        task_id: i64,
  1259→        event_type: &str,
  1260→        content: &str,
  1261→        metadata: Option<&serde_json::Value>,
  1262→    ) -> Result<AgentEvent> {
  1263→        let metadata_str = match metadata {
  1264→            Some(m) => {
  1265→                Some(serde_json::to_string(m).context("Failed to serialize event metadata")?)
  1266→            }
  1267→            None => None,
  1268→        };
  1269→        self.conn.execute(
  1270→            "INSERT INTO agent_events (task_id, event_type, content, metadata) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4)",
  1271→            params![task_id, event_type, content, metadata_str],
  1272→        ).context("Failed to insert agent event")?;
  1273→        let id = self.conn.last_insert_rowid();
  1274→        Ok(AgentEvent {
  1275→            id,
  1276→            task_id,
  1277→            event_type: {
  1278→                let val = event_type;
  1279→                val.parse()
  1280→                    .map_err(|_| anyhow::anyhow!("invalid event_type in database: '{}'", val))?
  1281→            },
  1282→            content: content.to_string(),
  1283→            metadata: metadata.cloned(),
  1284→            created_at: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
  1285→        })
  1286→    }
  1287→
  1288→    pub fn get_agent_events(
  1289→        &self,
  1290→        task_id: i64,
  1291→        limit: i64,
  1292→        offset: i64,
  1293→    ) -> Result<Vec<AgentEvent>> {
  1294→        let mut stmt = self
  1295→            .conn
  1296→            .prepare(
  1297→                "SELECT id, task_id, event_type, content, metadata, created_at \
  1298→             FROM agent_events WHERE task_id = ?1 ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT ?2 OFFSET ?3",
  1299→            )
  1300→            .context("Failed to prepare get_agent_events")?;
  1301→        let rows = stmt.query_map(params![task_id, limit, offset], |row| {
  1302→            Ok((
  1303→                row.get::<_, i64>(0)?,
  1304→                row.get::<_, i64>(1)?,
  1305→                row.get::<_, String>(2)?,
  1306→                row.get::<_, String>(3)?,
  1307→                row.get::<_, Option<String>>(4)?,
  1308→                row.get::<_, String>(5)?,
  1309→            ))
  1310→        })?;
  1311→        let mut events = Vec::new();
  1312→        for row in rows {
  1313→            let (id, task_id, event_type_str, content, metadata_str, created_at) = row?;
  1314→            let metadata: Option<serde_json::Value> =
  1315→                match metadata_str {
  1316→                    Some(s) => Some(serde_json::from_str(&s).map_err(|e| {
  1317→                        anyhow::anyhow!("corrupt event metadata JSON '{}': {}", s, e)
  1318→                    })?),
  1319→                    None => None,
  1320→                };
  1321→            events.push(AgentEvent {
  1322→                id,
  1323→                task_id,
  1324→                event_type: event_type_str.parse().map_err(|_| {
  1325→                    anyhow::anyhow!("invalid event_type in database: '{}'", event_type_str)
  1326→                })?,
  1327→                content,
  1328→                metadata,
  1329→                created_at,
  1330→            });
  1331→        }
  1332→        Ok(events)
  1333→    }

  1337→    pub fn get_setting(&self, key: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {
  1338→        let mut stmt = self
  1339→            .conn
  1340→            .prepare("SELECT value FROM settings WHERE key = ?1")
  1341→            .context("Failed to prepare get_setting")?;
  1342→        let mut rows = stmt
  1343→            .query_map(params![key], |row| row.get::<_, String>(0))
  1344→            .context("Failed to query setting")?;
  1345→        match rows.next() {
  1346→            Some(row) => Ok(Some(row.context("Failed to read setting")?)),
  1347→            None => Ok(None),
  1348→        }
  1349→    }
  1350→
  1351→    pub fn set_setting(&self, key: &str, value: &str) -> Result<()> {
  1352→        self.conn
  1353→            .execute(
  1354→                "INSERT INTO settings (key, value, updated_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, datetime('now'))
  1355→                 ON CONFLICT(key) DO UPDATE SET value = excluded.value, updated_at = datetime('now')",
  1356→                params![key, value],
  1357→            )
  1358→            .context("Failed to upsert setting")?;
  1359→        Ok(())
  1360→    }
  1361→
  1362→    pub fn delete_setting(&self, key: &str) -> Result<()> {
  1363→        self.conn
  1364→            .execute("DELETE FROM settings WHERE key = ?1", params![key])
  1365→            .context("Failed to delete setting")?;
  1366→        Ok(())
  1367→    }
